# TraderBro CLI Documentation

TraderBro is a command-line tool for querying financial analyst predictions,
return analytics, sector-level research, and content monitoring data.
Designed for humans and AI agents. Always use --json for machine-readable output.

## Key pages

- [Get Started](/getting-started/installation): Installation and authentication
- [CLI Reference](/cli-reference/overview): Complete command reference with flags, examples, and JSON output schemas
- [Analyst Commands](/cli-reference/analyst): analyst list, get, predictions, sector-edge, sector-map
- [Prediction Commands](/cli-reference/prediction): prediction list, get — with direction, date, and symbol filters
- [Symbol Commands](/cli-reference/symbol): symbol list, search, mentions, predictions
- [Content Commands](/cli-reference/content): content list, get — tweets, videos, articles
- [Research Commands](/cli-reference/research): research list, get — published research articles
- [Screener Commands](/cli-reference/screener): screener schema, values, run — filter stocks by fundamentals, valuation, price, and performance; use 'screener values <field>' to resolve exact categorical strings (sector, industry, exchange, country) before filtering; --symbols-only for piping into calculated-events scan
- [Prices Commands](/cli-reference/prices): traderbro prices NASDAQ:AAPL — daily OHLCV bars; flags: --from, --to, --within-days, --last, --json, --csv, --no-header; server caps at 5000 bars per request with self-describing pagination via "truncated" + "next_from"
- [Calculated Events Reference](/cli-reference/calculated-events): types, list, scan, refresh, patterns. The `patterns` subcommand returns per-event-type historical forward returns (1D/3D/7D/1M/3M/6M/1Y) for a single symbol — bearish returns are sign-flipped so higher always means "pattern played out." Use `--hot-only` for currently-relevant high-accuracy setups; see the `pattern-effectiveness` skill for workflow.
- [Return Calculation](/concepts/return-calculation): How direction-adjusted returns and time windows work
- [Accuracy Rate](/concepts/accuracy-rate): How directional accuracy is measured and when it appears
- [Sector Edge](/concepts/sector-edge): Per-analyst sector and industry performance breakdown
- [Finding Top Analysts](/guides/finding-top-analysts): Filter by country, period, accuracy, and return
- [Sector Edge Research](/guides/sector-edge-research): Use sector-edge and sector-map for stock selection
- [Scripting and Automation](/guides/scripting-and-automation): Shell scripts, cron jobs, jq pipelines
- [Using with AI Agents](/guides/using-with-ai-agents): Prompts, patterns, and workflow skills for Claude, GPT, and other AI assistants
- [Workflow Skills](/guides/using-with-ai-agents#workflow-skills): Step-by-step skill workflows embedded in the binary — run `traderbro skills list --json` to discover them

## Chart Control — traderbro.ai chart (traderbro brochart)

The proprietary-data chart: TraderBro's custom overlays (analyst marks, calculated events, predictions). Requires `traderbro brochart serve` running and the TraderBro chart page open in browser.

- [brochart Reference](/cli-reference/brochart): serve, state, symbol, screenshot, bars, draw, study (list/add/remove/clear/values), save, zoom, range, timeframe, search, close, charts, use, eval, health, refresh
- Standard workflow: symbol → range → close+screenshot → bars → draw → close+screenshot → save
- Always run `brochart close` before `brochart screenshot`; always run `brochart bars` before `brochart draw` to get authoritative timestamps
- For numeric questions (RSI value, MACD cross detection, divergence patterns), use `brochart study values` — returns bars + every active study's computed series time-aligned in one fetch
- `brochart bars` returns volume in the `v` field; `brochart study values --include-volume` hoists Volume to a top-level `volume` field
- For long-running agents (>20 symbols), use `brochart health` (read-only diagnostic) and `brochart refresh` (reload tab to reclaim memory or recover from stuck state). See the `tv-long-running` skill for the composition pattern.
- `brochart eval` is an escape hatch only — use structured commands first

## Chart Control — official tradingview.com chart (traderbro tvsandbox)

The high-fidelity price/volume path via Chrome DevTools Protocol on a dedicated, app-owned Chrome (port 9333, profile ~/.traderbro/chrome-profile). Deeper intraday, extended hours, long history, and bulk scanning. Sign in once with `traderbro tvsandbox login` (run `traderbro skills show tvsandbox-setup` first).

- [tvsandbox Reference](/cli-reference/tvsandbox): login, whoami, eval, bars, snap, frame, screenshot, screen, metrics, sweep, draw, clear
- `screen` calls TradingView's public scanner API directly — no browser, no login; everything else needs a signed-in session. One shared Chrome — run tvsandbox commands sequentially, never concurrently.
- **No pattern detector** — the agent judges bull/bear/continuation from bars + screenshot; server-side pattern detection is `traderbro calculated-events`.
- Workflow: `screen` (prefilter) → `metrics` (rank) → `sweep` (bulk per-symbol screenshot+bars for AI review) → read + decide; `draw <shape> --points` renders any of ~90 native TradingView objects (you supply the anchors), `clear` removes them
- Use `tvsandbox` for real intraday/extended-hours data, bulk scanning, and native drawing; use `brochart` for TraderBro's proprietary overlays; use `calculated-events` for pattern detection

## Global flags (apply to all commands)

--json          Output as JSON (use for all agent/script use)
--plain         Tab-delimited output for shell pipelines
--jq <expr>     Apply jq expression to JSON output (implies --json)
--limit <n>     Items per page, default 25, max 100
--page <n>      Page number, default 1
--quiet / -q    Suppress non-essential stderr
--verbose / -v  Show debug info on stderr

## Exit codes

0   Success
2   Authentication failure
3   Not found
4   Validation error (bad flag value)
5   Network error
